one_time_description: Specific Claims Settlement recognized as revenue in fiscal 2024
economic_notes: First Nation is a member of Meadow Lake Tribal Council (MLTC), an organization of nine nations. Holds 11.1% interest in MLTC Resource Development LP and 99% interest in MSFN Economic Development Company LP. Owns 100% of Makwa Lake Development Co and MSFN Economic Development Company Ltd. MSFN Construction Company LP is a related party. Country Choice Meats was purchased on behalf of MSFN Economic Development Company Ltd during the year.
notes: Large one-time Specific Claims Settlement of $15,000,000 recognized in 2024 significantly inflates revenue and surplus. Total government transfers estimated as sum of ISC ($25,997,123) + Ottawa Trust ($36,670) + MLTC ($5,621,760) + Saskatchewan Lotteries ($38,279) + CMHC ($5,009,013) + Northern Lights ($197,166) + First Nations Trust ($889,447) + Distribution from trust ($2,214,182) + Specific Claims Settlement ($15,000,000) + Recognition of unexpended funding ($2,365,780) + Sales tax refunds ($54,439) = ~$57,423,859 plus unexpended funding recognition; OSR includes store sales, rent, interest, LP earnings, GBE earnings, and other revenue. Consolidated entities include MSFN Operations, CMHC Housing Fund, and Store Enterprise. GBEs (Makwa Lake Development Co and MSFN Economic Development Company Ltd) and LPs (MLTC Resource Development LP and MSFN Economic Development Company LP) accounted for under modified equity method. CIBC Trust Loan of $2,417,721 taken to fund specific claims settlement, repayable May 2024. Amortization per TCA schedule is $1,866,936 vs $1,866,336 on income statement (minor rounding difference). No schedule of remuneration included in the provided text.
